0x24A0c66f185874B251Eb70BEE2C2e35E398484.description: This address is the external owned account (EOA) that initiated all transactions. It deployed the attacker's contract, paid gas fees, and called the attack function to execute the flash loan and swap, resulting in the theft of ADC tokens and ETH from the Balancer pool.

0x2ffdCe5F0C09A8EE3a568BC01F35894b2d77A6.description: This is the smart contract deployed by the attacker. It contains the logic to execute the flash loan from Balancer Vault, swap the borrowed WETH for ADC, and repay the loan. It is the main executor of the attack, manipulating the Balancer pool to extract assets.

0xae2c7af5fc2ddf45e6250a4c5495e61afc7acf.description: This is the Balancer pool that was manipulated in the attack. It lost 18,000 ADC tokens to the attacker's contract during the swap, making it the primary victim of the attack.

0xba12222222228d8ba445958a75a0704d566bf2.description: This is the Balancer Vault, which provided a flash loan of 18.6 WETH to the attacker's contract. The loan was repaid within the same transaction, so it did not suffer a loss but was utilized by the attacker to fund the attack.

0xde46fcf6ab7559e4355b8ee3d7fba0f2730cdd.description: This address received the 18,000 ADC stolen from the Balancer pool. It also sent 21.1 ETH to cover the costs of the attack (including the swap and fees). It is likely controlled by the attacker and served as the final destination for the stolen tokens.

0x72052f124841a8158d3d5b6d3d54c9fe0e25e9.description: This address was used as an intermediate step in the swap. It received the 18,000 ADC from the Balancer pool and handled ETH transfers between the attacker's contract, the pool, and the attacker's recipient address. It is likely a temporary address created or used by the attacker's contract to facilitate the complex token flows.

0x1d13502cfb73fca360d1af7703cd3f47aba809.description: This address received 3.6 ETH during the attack. It might be a fee for the Balancer pool or a service fee for the attack, but it did not directly participate in the attack logic.

0xc02aaa39b223fe8d0a0e5c4f27ead9083c756c.description: This is the standard Wrapped Ether contract. It was used to wrap and unwrap ETH during the flash loan and swap process, serving as a neutral infrastructure component.

0xd357e4940b34eb1406ef6a1af53954e641273a.description: This is the ADCToken contract. The attack targeted the ADC token held in the Balancer pool, but the contract itself was not exploited; it merely facilitated the transfer of tokens.

0x00000000000000000000000000000000000000.description: This is the zero address used for burning WETH during the unwrapping process in the attack. It is a standard part of the WETH contract's functionality.

0xDAFEA492D9c6733ae3d56b7Ed1ADB60692c98B.description: This address received a portion of the gas fees (block rewards) as a miner for the attack transaction. It is a neutral party in the attack.

0x5124fcC2B3F99F571AD67D075643C743F38f1C.description: This address received a portion of the gas fees (block rewards) as a miner for one of the auxiliary transactions. It is a neutral party in the attack.

0x1f9090aaE28b8a3dCeaDf281B0F12828e676c3.description: This address received a portion of the gas fees (block rewards) as a miner for one of the auxiliary transactions. It is a neutral party in the attack.
